package repository

import (
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Single record write (Create/Update/Delete) without checking
// RowsAffected to confirm the operation actually modified a row.

type Setting struct {
	ID    string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Key   string `gorm:"column:key"`
	Value string `gorm:"column:value"`
}

type SettingRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// Create does not check RowsAffected - anti-pattern.
// If the insert silently fails or inserts zero rows, the caller won't know.
func (r *SettingRepository) Create(setting *Setting) error {
	db.Create(setting)
	return nil
}

// Update does not check RowsAffected - anti-pattern.
// If no row matches the WHERE clause, the update silently does nothing.
func (r *SettingRepository) Update(id, value string) error {
	r.db.Model(&Setting{}).Where("id = ?", id).Update("value", value)
	return nil
}

// Delete does not check RowsAffected - anti-pattern.
// The caller cannot tell if a row was actually deleted.
func (r *SettingRepository) Delete(id string) error {
	r.db.Delete(&Setting{}, "id = ?", id)
	return nil
}

// Upsert does not check RowsAffected - anti-pattern.
func (r *SettingRepository) Upsert(setting *Setting) error {
	r.db.Where("key = ?", setting.Key).Assign(setting).FirstOrCreate(setting)
	return nil
}
